revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iibonimasuamaterasu-jinja]][[ja:粒坐天照神社]] {{Expand Japanese|粒坐天照神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine | native_name = 粒坐天照神社 | image = Iiboniamaterasu-jinja Tatsuno Hyogo pref05n4272.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.863615|134.5403}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amenohoakari|lt=honoakari|en|Amenohoakari|fr|Ame-no-hoakari|it|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|ko|아메노호아카리|mni|ꯑꯃꯦꯅꯣꯍꯣꯑꯀꯔꯤ|simple|Ame-no-hoakari|zh|天火明命|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} | established = {'time': '+0594-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine'''|粒坐天照神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498723|ltq=Q11498723|qid=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} ** Series ordinal: 12 ** P155: {{ill|Takamikura Shrine|lt=Takamikura Shrine|qid=Q135041151|1=Takamikura Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q17191369|ltq=Q17191369|qid=Q17191369|1=Asō Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q877190|ltq=Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Harima Province|lt=Harima Province|be|Харыма (правінцыя)|ca|Província de Harima|ceb|Harima (baranggay sa Hapon)|cs|Provincie Harima|de|Provinz Harima|en|Harima Province|envoyage|Harima|es|Provincia de Harima|fa|ولایت هاریما|fr|Province de Harima|gl|Provincia de Harima|id|Provinsi Harima|it|Harima (provincia)|ja|播磨国|ko|하리마국|lt|Harimos žemė|nl|Harima (provincie)|pt|Província de Harima|ru|Харима (провинция)|simple|Harima Province|sk|Harima (provincia)|sr|Провинција Харима|tr|Harima (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Харіма|zh_min_nan|Harima-kok|zh|播磨國|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Q1042150|ltq=Q1042150|qid=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.863615|134.5403}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == inception (P571) == * {'time': '+0594-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == image (P18) == * Iiboniamaterasu-jinja Tatsuno Hyogo pref05n4272.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q10940411|ltq=Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20130613152215/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/440301.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183018 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いいぼにますあまてらすじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005006643 == Commons category (P373) == * Iibonimasuamaterasu-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県たつの市龍野町日山449-1', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Iiboniamaterasu-jinja Tatsuno Hyogo pref02n4272.jpg {{commons category|Category:Iibonimasuamaterasu-jinja}} [[JA:粒坐天照神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11604472}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine |Image = [[File:Iibonimasu-amaterasu-jinja, keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine grounds |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}449-1 Hiyama, Tatsuno-chō |Coordinates = {{Wikicoordinates2|34|51|49.6|N|134|32|25.1|E|region:JP-28_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hiko Hoakari no Kami|1=Amenohoakari}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=UNKNOWN}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=594|Q33625|qid=Q33625|1=594}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = One of the Three Great Shrines of Harima |Annual festival = The day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Iibonimasu
Amaterasu Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iiboniamaterasu-jinja Tatsuno Hyogo pref12n4272.jpg|thumb|170px|Shrine grounds]] '''Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine''' (いいぼにますあまてらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located on Mount Hiyama (Hakuroyama) in {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and was ranked as a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|qid=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} and {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|Q11559098|qid=Q11559098|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|1=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)}}, it is collectively known as the “Three Great Shrines of Harima.” The mountain where the shrine stands is said to resemble the shape of a seated dragon, from which its former name, '''Ryūza Shrine''' (“Dragon Seat Shrine”), derives—a name still widely used locally. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hiko Hoakari no Kami|1=Amenohoakari}}“According to tradition, the enshrined deity of this shrine is Amateru Kuniteru Hiko Hoakari no Mikoto. To the west of this shrine, in Isei Village, stands {{ill|lt=Iseki Sansha Shrine|Q17193121|qid=Q17193121|1=Isekisan Shrine}}, whose deity is Amateru Kuniteru Hiko Kushitama Nigihayahi no Mikoto. Although these two deities appear distinct, the {{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|lt=Kujiki|1=Kujiki}} identifies Nigihayahi as Hoakari.” (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer). For a period, Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine enshrined Amaterasu Ōmikami as the principal deity in the main hall, and under the influence of State Shinto after the Meiji period, adopted the reading “Amaterasu Shrine.” By contrast, Iseki Sansha Shrine retains the older reading “Amateru Shrine” and distinguishes its deity from Amaterasu Ōmikami. The Kujiki, however, treats Hoakari and Nigihayahi as the same deity. is enshrined here, and the sacred tree is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=cedar|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === The shrine is said to have been founded in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=594|Q33625|qid=Q33625|1=594}}) at the summit of Mount Hiyama ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakuroyama|1=UNKNOWN}}). Today, the shrine grounds extend from the mountainside down to the foothills. The name “Iibo” (粒) is considered the origin of the place-name “Ibo” (揖保)However, the claim that Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine is the origin of this etymology has only been asserted in relatively recent times; traditionally, {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine#Legend of Tsubu Hill and Shikinaisha Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine|Q17226544|qid=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} has been regarded as the stronger candidate.. During the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}Alternative theories appear in works such as “Jingishi Ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo-gun Chishi,” which argue for a different location.. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Sekiguchi Hisanori, formerly a ceremonial advisor to the {{ill|lt=Tatsuno Domain|Q1012712|qid=Q1012712|1=Tatsuno Domain}}, was appointed priest by order of {{ill|lt=Wakisaka Yasushi|Q11612111|qid=Q11612111|1=Yasuaya Wakisaka}}, then {{ill|lt=Governor|Q11584647|qid=Q11584647|1=domainal governor}}, to revive the shrine as the guardian shrine of Tatsuno under {{ill|lt=State Shinto|Q1197248|qid=Q1197248|1=State Shinto}}. Sekiguchi Kumi, an authority in {{ill|lt=vibration engineering|Q11498075|qid=Q11498075|1=vibration engineering}} and former president of {{ill|lt=Himeji Institute of Technology|Q11447475|qid=Q11447475|1=Himeji Kōgyō Daigaku}}, was the fourth-generation head of the shrine family and served concurrently as priest. The shrine continues to be maintained by their descendants and related families. In the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in the 15th year of Meiji it was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. The shrine’s parishioners number about 3,300 households, primarily in Tatsuno-chō on both banks of the {{ill|lt=Ibo River|Q11498396|qid=Q11498396|1=Ibo River}}, including areas such as Hiyama, the old castle town of Tatsuno, Kita-Tatsuno, and parts of Isei and Ibogawa. === Chronology === ; Shikinaisha: Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine * 2nd year of Empress Suiko (594): Enshrined near the summit of Matoba-yama * {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}}): Granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}): Granted Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=Yōwa|Q729609|qid=Q729609|1=Yōwa}} 1 (1181): When 174 shrines of Harima Province were consolidated at {{ill|lt=Itate Hyōzu Shrine|Q11458547|qid=Q11458547|1=Itatehyōzu Shrine}} to form the provincial {{ill|lt=Sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, this shrine was not included, and its buildings are thought to have already been lost, with rites discontinuedThe “Harima Kuni Jinmyōchō” lists Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine as “Nakatomi Tsubu Ōkami,” suggesting a former consolidation. ; Hiyama Shrine * Early history unknown * Damaged in the {{ill|lt=Ōei Rebellion|Q10285774|qid=Q10285774|1=Ōei Rebellion}} * {{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|qid=Q1193238|1=Kakitsu}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|qid=Q6402|1=1441}}): Destroyed in the {{ill|lt=Kakitsu Incident|Q5365139|qid=Q5365139|1=Kakitsu incident}} * Subsequently relocated and a small shrine established on the former mountain site * {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1471|Q6577|qid=Q6577|1=1471}}): Relocated to the present site by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Akamatsu Norishige|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1581|Q6717|qid=Q6717|1=1581}}): Rebuilt as '''Ryūza Shrine''' by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachisuka Masakatsu|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|qid=Q6738|1=1604}}): Destroyed by fire * {{ill|lt=Kan’ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1626|Q6831|qid=Q6831|1=1626}}): Rebuilt * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}}): Renamed '''Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine''' * {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q2082716|qid=Q2082716|1=Pfarrkirche Itter}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|qid=Q5240|1=1945}}): Main hall destroyed by fire === Shrine legend === During the reigns of the 32nd emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q349238|qid=Q349238|1=Emperor Sushun}} and the 33rd {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}, there lived in what is now Tatsuno City a wealthy and virtuous man named Ifukube no Muraji Hakata-hiko. On the first day of the New Year in the 2nd year of Suiko, a strange light appeared above a grove behind his residence. It transformed into a beautiful child who declared himself a messenger of Amateru Kuniteru Hiko Hoakari no Mikoto and bestowed divine instructions and sacred rice seeds. After the seeds were planted, the land prospered, yielding abundant harvests, and the area came to be known as “Iibo,” meaning rice grain. In gratitude, the people enshrined the deity and named the shrine Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine, venerating it as their tutelary shrine to this day. === Rituals === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chinowa-nuke Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} – July 14–15 * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} – The Sunday before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October === Shrine grounds === * Main hall * Worship hall * Ema hall * Shrine gate * Inner shrine – Tentsushi Shrine * Middle shrine – Komiya Shrine * Auxiliary shrines within the grounds * Auxiliary shrines outside the grounds === Access === * 25-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|qid=Q11520740|1=Hon-Tatsuno Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|qid=Q869221|1=Kishin Line}} === Surroundings === * {{ill|lt=Nishinomiyama Kofun|Q125960617|qid=Q125960617|1=Nishinomiyama Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Hakuroyama Park|Q11580636|qid=Q11580636|1=Shirasagi-yama Park}} * Tatsuno High School * {{ill|lt=Higashimaru Shōyu|Q11102824|qid=Q11102824|1=Higashimaru Shoyu}} Second Factory === References === * Toshihiko Nishio & Hiroshi Fujimaki, *A Small Encyclopedia of Native Japanese Rice Varieties*, Rural Culture Association of Japan, 2020. === External links === * Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association) * Iibonimasu Amaterasu Shrine (Kokugakuin University, Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tatsuno, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Tatsuno, Hyogo]]
